exercises.title: Analyze Your Own Deployment
exercises.description: Apply the case study framework to analyze a quantum deployment scenario.
tasks: Choose a quantum application scenario (VQE for chemistry, QAOA for optimization, or QML)
Document the business context including problem, value proposition, and success metrics
Design the technical architecture with backend selection and error mitigation
Define operational approach including SLOs and monitoring
Identify potential challenges and mitigation strategies
exercises.expectedOutput: Complete CaseStudy data structure with all fields populated
hints: Use the CaseStudy dataclass as a template
Be realistic about challenges based on the case studies reviewed
Include specific SLO targets based on the application type

exercises.title: Incident Post-Mortem
exercises.description: Create a detailed post-incident review for a simulated quantum production incident.
tasks: Design a realistic incident scenario (e.g., backend failure, result corruption)
Create a detailed timeline from first symptoms to resolution
Perform root cause analysis using the 5 Whys technique
Document lessons learned and identify systemic issues
Create action items with owners and due dates
Generate the post-incident review document
exercises.expectedOutput: Complete IncidentReport with detailed analysis and action items
hints: Good post-mortems are blameless and focus on systemic issues
Include both what went wrong and what went right
Action items should be specific and measurable

exercises.title: Build Pattern Catalog
exercises.description: Extend the deployment patterns catalog with patterns from your experience or research.
tasks: Identify 3 additional deployment patterns (either patterns or anti-patterns)
Document each using the DeploymentPattern structure
Provide real or hypothetical examples for each pattern
Explain the trade-offs and when to apply each pattern
Create a pattern selection guide for common scenarios
exercises.expectedOutput: Extended patterns list with new patterns and selection guide
hints: Consider patterns from other domains that apply to quantum
Anti-patterns are often discovered through painful experience
Good patterns should be actionable, not just descriptive

exercises.title: Deployment Readiness Assessment
exercises.description: Use the decision framework to assess deployment readiness for a scenario.
tasks: Define a specific quantum application deployment scenario
Score each of the 5 criteria categories (0-1 scale) with justification
Use the DeploymentDecisionFramework to evaluate readiness
Identify blocking issues and generate prioritized next steps
Create a remediation plan for the lowest-scoring areas
exercises.expectedOutput: Complete readiness assessment with scores, recommendation, and remediation plan
hints: Be honest in scoring - overestimating readiness leads to problems
Blocking issues (score < 0.5) must be addressed before production
Next steps should be specific and achievable
